Section 1  
The function analyzes the health improvements reported by participants in different age groups.  

Section 2  
The function uses a dictionary to store the most common health improvement for each age group.  

Section 3  
The function counts the occurrences of each health improvement for each age group.  

Section 4  
The function returns a dictionary with the most common health improvement for each age group.  

Section 5  
The function ensures that the output is structured and easy to read.  

Section 6  
The function is designed to handle different age groups and their respective health improvements.  

Section 7  
The function is efficient and works well with the provided input format.  

* The function processes the input list and counts the occurrences of each health improvement for each age group.  
* The function returns the most common health improvement for each age group in a structured dictionary.
